title: Xylosma nelsonii
intro: Xylosma nelsonii, also known as Nelson's xylosma, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Salicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Xylosma nelsonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been found to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Xylosma nelsonii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions and other health ailments.
